revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:愛宕神社 (天童市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Atago-jinja (Tendō city) honden.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Tendo|qid=Q385779|1=Tendō, Yamagata}}|coordinates={{Coord|38.35063888888889|N|140.37877777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}}}
]] '''Atago Shrine''' (愛宕神社) == Enshrined Deity == * '''{{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} (Homusubi-no-Kami)''' == History == The origins of Atago Shrine date back to May 1, 1377 (Eiwa 3), when {{ill|Satomi Yorinao|ja|里見頼直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomi Yorinao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the then-lord of {{ill|Tendō Castle|ja|天童城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendō Castle|1=Tendō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11443358}}, enshrined {{ill|Shōgun Jizō|ja|勝軍地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgun Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q604687}} in the inner chamber of his palace. On December 24, 1584 (Tenshō 12), after {{ill|Mogami Yoshiaki|en|Mogami Yoshiaki|qid=Q839377|lt=Mogami Yoshiaki|1=Mogami Yoshiaki}}, the lord of {{ill|Yamagata Castle|en|Yamagata Castle|qid=Q15604669|lt=Yamagata Castle|1=Yamagata Castle}}, conquered Tendō Castle, he established the shrine on the former site of the castle’s main keep and donated 1,370 koku of land. This event is considered the official founding of the shrine. The following year, on June 24, 1585 (Tenshō 13), the guardian deity of the Satomi clan, Shōgun Jizō, was transferred to this location, and a grand festival was held. The shrine buildings were destroyed by fire later that same year. They were rebuilt in 1603 (Keichō 8) but were destroyed again by a violent storm during the Kanbun era. The current structures were constructed in 1678 (Enpō 6). During the Edo period, the shrine was said to oversee 48 subordinate shrines. With the establishment of the Modern Shrine Ranking System during the Meiji period, Atago Shrine was designated a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine (Honden)''': Reconstructed in 1678 (Enpō 6). The carvings of pine, bamboo, plum, and phoenix motifs, along with the richly colored paintings of hawks, pine trees, and camellias on the inner walls, are believed to have been created in Kyoto, retaining elements of Momoyama culture. The vibrant depictions of guardian lions on the sliding doors were painted by artists of the {{ill|Kanō school|en|Kanō school|qid=Q252801|lt=Kano school|1=Kanō school}}. Designated as a tangible cultural property of Tendō City in 1971 (Shōwa 46). == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Tendō City === * '''Atago Shrine Main Hall''' - Designated in 1971 (Shōwa 46). * '''Color on Paper Depiction of a Divine Horse (Shinmeizu)''' - Created during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q319531}}. This votive tablet (ema), measuring 188 cm by 212 cm, depicts a dynamic image of an untamed horse controlled by an attendant. It was dedicated by {{ill|Mogami Ienobu|ja|最上家信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogami Ienobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 最上義俊|1=Mogami Ienobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8514354}}, the grandson of Mogami Yoshiaki, with the inscription "Dedicated for the fulfillment of various wishes." * '''Stone Lantern Shaft''' - The remaining shaft of a stone lantern originally dedicated in 1609 (Keichō 14) by {{ill|Ujiie Mitsuuji|ja|氏家光氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiie Mitsuuji|1=Ujiie Akiuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11128767}}. == Location == * 988-1 Kitame, Tendō City, Yamagata Prefecture, Japan == Related Pages == * {{ill|Tendō Shrine|ja|天童神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Also located atop {{ill|Mt. Maizuru|ja|舞鶴山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Maizuru|1=Mount Maizuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613695}} {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} {{Atago Faith|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|愛宕神社 (天童市)|version=85166608|comment=Imported full ja history}}
